name: James Washington
chief_complaint: Increased dyspnea and productive cough for 4 days
pmh: COPD, GOLD stage 3 (severe) — on home oxygen 2L/min
Obstructive sleep apnea on CPAP (8 cmH2O)
Former smoker — 45 pack-years, quit 8 years ago
Atrial fibrillation (rate-controlled, not on anticoagulation — prior GI bleed)
current_medications: Azithromycin 250mg daily (macrolide prophylaxis for COPD exacerbations)

allergies.reaction: GI bleed (upper GI bleed 2022 — attributed to aspirin use)

ed_assessment: 72-year-old male with severe COPD on home O2 2L presenting with 4 days of worsening dyspnea and productive yellow-green sputum. Reports running out of albuterol 3 days ago. Denies fever at home. Exam: accessory muscle use, prolonged expiratory phase, diffuse bilateral expiratory wheezing, no crackles. O2 sat 84% on room air, improved to 90% on 2L (his home flow rate), 93% on 4L. CXR shows hyperinflation with no new consolidation, no pneumothorax. ABG on 2L: pH 7.34, pCO2 58 (elevated from baseline ~52), pO2 62, bicarb 32. Acute-on-chronic hypercapnic respiratory failure consistent with COPD exacerbation. Starting IV methylprednisolone, ipratropium and albuterol nebulizers q4h, and doxycycline for presumed infectious exacerbation. Pulmonology paged. Monitor closely for worsening — low threshold for BiPAP if pCO2 continues to rise.
handoff_notes: 72M severe COPD exac, acute-on-chronic hypercapnic failure. On 4L NC — target O2 sat 88-92% only (hypercapnic driver). Steroids started. On doxycycline — sputum culture pending. Serial ABGs ordered. On telemetry for afib monitoring. NOT a BiPAP candidate per patient preference (completed POLST — comfort-focused measures, no intubation). Pulmonology to see in AM. Family meeting scheduled for tomorrow.
baseline_functional_status: Prior to this admission: severely limited — unable to walk more than 20 feet without stopping. Dependent on home O2 24h/day. Uses a rollator walker. Lives with son. Unable to climb stairs. Sleeps in recliner. Prior hospitalization for COPD exacerbation 5 months ago required 6-day stay with BiPAP. POLST on file: DNI, trial of BiPAP acceptable but patient has been reconsidering.

imaging.indication: COPD exacerbation — evaluate for consolidation, pneumothorax
imaging.findings: Marked bilateral hyperinflation. Flattened hemidiaphragms. No focal consolidation. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. Mild cardiomegaly. Findings consistent with known severe COPD.
imaging.impression: Severe COPD without acute infiltrate or pneumothorax. No radiographic evidence of pneumonia.

prior_hospitalizations.treatment: IV steroids, nebulizers, doxycycline, non-invasive BiPAP overnight × 2 nights. Discharged home on prednisone taper, doxycycline course, and home oxygen uptitrated to 2L.
prior_hospitalizations.transitional_issues: Pulmonology follow-up in 2 weeks. Discussed pulmonary rehab enrollment — patient willing. POLST completed this admission.

prior_hospitalizations.treatment: IV ceftriaxone, azithromycin, steroids. Required BiPAP for 3 days. Started on macrolide prophylaxis at discharge.
prior_hospitalizations.transitional_issues: Repeat CXR in 6 weeks to confirm pneumonia resolution. Goals of care discussion deferred to outpatient setting.
